title: k_thread_name_set return value not checked
description:
In lora_l2.c lichen_lora_l2_start(), k_thread_name_set() return value is ignored.

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:263

Code: k_thread_name_set(&rx_thread_data, "lora_rx");

What goes wrong: k_thread_name_set() can return -EINVAL (invalid thread) or -ENOSYS (thread naming disabled). Failure to set a debug name is benign - the thread still runs correctly - but ignoring the return violates the 'check all return codes' guideline.

In practice, this call cannot fail:
- The thread was just created on line 259
- CONFIG_THREAD_NAME must be enabled for this call to exist

Options:
1. Cast to (void) to document intentional ignore
2. Remove the call if CONFIG_THREAD_NAME is not enabled
3. Log warning on failure

Filing as P2 since failure has no functional impact.
